channel.title: Open Governance on Ivan Fraixedes' Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Open Governance on Ivan Fraixedes' Blog

item.title: My 5 favourite tweets of the week #3
item.description:
This week I got my 5 favourite tweets of my twitter timeline almost the last two days; I’ve also been very busy this week, however I walked through in my timeline at the end of the each day and I’ve selected:

io.js team is working very hard, the open governance applied to the project, one of the main reason they forked NodeJS, seems that works quite well and they’re moving forward pretty fast; this tweet talks itself about it

io.js v2.x is probably closer than you think https://t.co/mXYpmyRQW0

— Rod Vagg.js (@rvagg) March 4, 2015

Quite amazing MIT open source project witch brings the ability to compile and execute c code in one go

C – Compile and execute C “scripts” in one go https://t.co/Q7pzgO5Ku9

— Hacker News (@newsycombinator) March 4, 2015

I’ve discovered parse5, an npm module released under MIT as well, which I considered that it looks interesting

Parse5: WHATWG HTML5-Compliant HTML Parsing/Serialization Toolset for Node - http://t.co/atODyW5Y0m

— JavaScript Daily (@JavaScriptDaily) March 4, 2015

One disclosed bug in OpenSSL always is quite interesting due the number of apps, systems, libraries, etc, which use it, however the versions with this issues are older than the version used by io.js and NodeJS production releases (0.12.0 and 0.10.36)

Are #nodejs and #iojs affected by the “FREAK Attack” OpenSSL vulnerability? http://t.co/DQi5sgDUev

— StrongLoop (@StrongLoop) March 3, 2015

And lastly I’ve selected a tweet which brought me to read a research experiment done by Mozilla; definitely, it deserves to have a read

The Emterpreter: Running Code Before It Can Be Parsed - https://t.co/MYU8VuElOn

— JavaScript Daily (@JavaScriptDaily) March 3, 2015

And that’s it of my part, have you got any interesting tweet that you’d like to share? drop it in a comment, I would like to read it.
